source: CAS Academic Policies §College Credits Taken in Secondary School (line 320-326)
body:
**CAS first-year matriculants get at most 32 advanced standing credits combined.**

From the CAS bulletin (§College Credits Taken in Secondary School):

*"Note also that students who enter CAS as first years may be awarded no more than 32 advanced standing credits; this limit includes both credits from Advanced Placement and similar examinations and previous college credits."*

What counts toward the 32-credit cap:
- Advanced Placement (AP) examinations
- International Baccalaureate (IB) and A-Level / similar accepted exams
- College credits earned via dual enrollment (community college or 4-year university)

Carve-outs:
- *"Note that students who matriculate as first years will not receive any transfer credit for post-secondary expository writing courses, whether or not they were taken as dual enrollment. The exact same policy applies to any mathematics courses below the level of precalculus."*
- AP/exam credits in social sciences and humanities **cannot** apply toward Foundations of Contemporary Culture (Core Curriculum) requirements.

If you're at or near 32 credits and considering more, talk to your CAS adviser — additional credits won't apply, even if they appear on your transfer-credit roll-up.
